questions.question:
Create a namespace named `secure-workloads` and enforce the Pod Security Admission `restricted` profile using the latest version labels.

In that namespace, create a Pod named `restricted-shell` using the image `busybox:1.36`.

The Pod must:
- run the command `sleep 3600`
- set `runAsNonRoot=true`
- run as UID `1000`
- set the seccomp profile to `RuntimeDefault`
- prevent privilege escalation
- drop all Linux capabilities

verification.description: Pod security context matches restricted requirements

questions.question:
DNS resolution is failing for workloads in the namespace `dns-lab`.

A deployment `web`, service `web`, test Pod `dns-check`, and a dedicated `CoreDNS` deployment `coredns` already exist in `dns-lab`.

Diagnose and fix the `CoreDNS` configuration in `dns-lab` so that:
- `dns-check` can resolve `web.dns-lab.svc.cluster.local`
- `dns-check` can fetch HTTP content from the `web` service

Do not recreate the application resources in `dns-lab`. Focus on restoring the `CoreDNS` resources used by that namespace.

verification.description: Dedicated CoreDNS deployment in dns-lab serves cluster.local and is available
dns-check can resolve the service FQDN
dns-check can reach the web service over HTTP

questions.question:
Install the `ingress-nginx` controller using Helm.

Requirements:
- add the repo `https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx`
- install the release with name `ingress-nginx` in namespace `ingress-nginx`
- configure the controller service type as `NodePort`
- set the HTTP `nodePort` to `30080`

A Deployment `web-app`, Service `web-service`, and a broken Ingress `web-ingress` already exist in namespace `ingress-lab`.

Fix the Ingress so requests with host `app.example.local` and path `/` route correctly to `web-service` on port `80`.

verification.description: ingress-nginx controller is installed and ready
Ingress uses host app.example.local and path / to route to web-service
